item.title: Walking the dog with Claude 摘要
item.description:
Matt Godbolt discusses the gap between accurate-but-impenetrable and polished-but-wrong technical content. He cites examples like Practical Engineering, LegalEagle, and Numberphile as bridges. He explains his 'tell my mum' test for explanations and the Usborne robot mental model he still uses for microarchitecture. He reflects on his motivations: showing off and the joy of someone 'getting it'. He discusses the costs of video creation (hours vs. conceptual distillation) and why he skipped a Computerphile recording. He is surprised by the idea that Compiler Explorer shares the same impulse as his talks and videos.
标签: technical communication, explanation, education, YouTube, Computerphile, Compiler Explorer, mental models, microarchitecture, C++, LLM interview

item.title: Mad House — Usborne Creepy Computer Games 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison discovered that UK publisher Usborne offers free PDFs of their 1980s computer books, including 'Creepy Computer Games' (1983). He fondly recalls typing in the 'Mad House' game on his Commodore 64 as a child. Using Claude, he fed the PDF and had it generate an interactive JavaScript/HTML version of the game with a retro aesthetic, mobile-friendly design, and proper credit to the book. The result is a playable browser-based recreation of the classic text-adventure game.
标签: retro-gaming, computer-history, games, AI, Claude, JavaScript, HTML, PDF, nostalgia, tools

item.title: Quoting Armin Ronacher 摘要
item.description:
Armin Ronacher criticizes the trend of AI-generated 'slop' in open-source issue reports, where users submit problems reworded by LLMs without personal observation. These reports often contain inaccurate conclusions, fake minimal reproductions, and irrelevant suggestions. He advocates for issue reports to be condensed to what the human actually observed: the command run, expected behavior, actual outcome, and exact error or log. This ensures clarity and accuracy in debugging.
标签: AI, open-source, github-issues, LLMs, AI-ethics, coding-agents, generative-ai, slop, bug-reporting, software-quality
